response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and systems engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and distributed systems exposure, but he falls critically short on the role's hard requirements. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis sound server project — nowhere near the threshold. Similarly, there is no demonstrated experience in cloud compute platform leadership, no cloud architecture ownership at scale, and no evidence of leading a platform engineering team. His mentoring and community contributions are genuine positives, but they do not compensate for the structural mismatch on the primary technical requirements.
strengths: Meaningful distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-performance design
Consistent track record of software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) and knowledge-sharing through public workshops and talks
Breadth across infrastructure tooling including Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker, and agentic/MCP developer tooling at re:cinq
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI plus a thesis project) against an 8-year hard requirement that is central to the role
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform; cloud work is limited to AWS CDK usage within a product team and a personal GISCollective Kubernetes deployment
No formal technical leadership or people-mentoring role in a cloud/infrastructure context; mentoring appears limited to community workshops and coding dojos
